macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin slips under $77K as markets digest macro shocks and ETF demand
items.summary: Bitcoin fell below the $77,000 level in the past day, with reports tying the move to rising Treasury yields and broader risk-off pressure. Several market roundups also noted sharp liquidations and short-term holder selling as BTC traded around the mid-$76K to $77K range.

items.title: Strategy buys $2 billion of Bitcoin in a single week
items.summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy reportedly added $2 billion in Bitcoin over the last week, reinforcing the company’s aggressive treasury strategy even as prices remained volatile. The purchase was among the most widely cited institutional crypto developments in the past 24 hours.

items.title: U.S. Senate Banking Committee advances major crypto market structure legislation
items.summary: Multiple crypto news trackers reported that the Senate Banking Committee advanced the Clarity Act, a major market-structure bill for digital assets. The move was highlighted as one of the biggest regulatory developments of the day and could reshape U.S. crypto oversight if it continues through Congress.

items.title: SEC unveils major overhaul to public-offering rules and reporting requirements
items.summary: The SEC announced transformative reforms aimed at simplifying registered offerings and reducing reporting burdens for public companies. Crypto markets are watching closely because the overhaul could affect how digital-asset firms access public markets and raise capital.

items.title: Stablecoin infrastructure and rails attract fresh funding
items.summary: Checker raised $8 million to build stablecoin infrastructure, adding to a wave of investment in payment and settlement rails for digital dollars. The funding news comes as stablecoin supply reportedly surpassed $300 billion, underscoring accelerating market growth.

items.title: Japan’s ruling party backs on-chain finance and AI-blockchain policy plans
items.summary: Japanese political leaders advanced proposals to integrate blockchain-based finance with AI-related systems, including a plan aimed at strengthening the yen’s digital future. The initiative adds to a growing list of government-led blockchain policy efforts in Asia.

items.title: Tor Project launches Web3 crowdfunding drive
items.summary: The Tor Project announced a Web3 crowdfunding campaign to support internet-freedom initiatives, marking a notable cross-over between privacy advocacy and blockchain fundraising. The effort was highlighted alongside other infrastructure and privacy-focused crypto developments.

items.title: Hyperliquid hits its highest level since October
items.summary: HYPE surged to its highest price since October near $48, with bullish commentary framing Hyperliquid as a potential crypto 'super app.' The move stood out among altcoin gains even as the broader market remained weak.

items.title: Dogecoin ETF demand spikes despite Elon Musk silence
items.summary: Dogecoin-linked ETF activity reportedly jumped 215% in the U.S., suggesting continued retail and speculative demand even without fresh support from Elon Musk. The move comes as DOGE price action remains sensitive to broader market momentum.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta hires four more OpenAI researchers, report says
items.summary: Meta has reportedly hired four additional OpenAI researchers as competition for top AI talent intensifies. The move underscores the continued battle among leading AI labs and tech companies to attract researchers with frontier-model expertise.

items.title: OpenAI’s ChatGPT experiences downtime as users report loading issues
items.summary: Users reported problems loading prompts in ChatGPT, indicating a service disruption at OpenAI. The incident highlights the operational fragility of widely used AI services as demand remains high.

items.title: Apple reportedly plans to open its underlying AI technology to developers
items.summary: Apple is said to be preparing broader access to its core AI technology for third-party developers. If confirmed, the move could expand the Apple AI ecosystem and accelerate app integration with its models and tools.

items.title: US-UAE multi-billion-dollar AI data campus deal remains unsettled, sources say
items.summary: A major proposed AI data campus deal between the U.S. and the UAE is still not finalized, according to sources. The uncertainty reflects how geopolitics, infrastructure, and compute access are shaping the global AI buildout.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Google I/O highlights include major Search and XR updates, plus new Gemini features
items.summary: Google unveiled a broad set of product updates at its annual developer conference, including major changes to Search, new Gemini-related tools, and updates for XR devices. The announcements also included smartglasses teasers from Samsung and Google and other consumer-facing product news.

items.title: Google expands Circle to Search with AI-generated image detection
items.summary: Google is bringing its image-verification capability to more products, allowing users to check whether an image was generated or edited with AI. The feature is part of a wider update to Google’s search and mobile ecosystem.

items.title: Samsung and Google tease upcoming Android XR smartglasses
items.summary: Samsung and Google previewed new Android XR smartglasses developed with Warby Parker and Gentle Monster. The teaser signals continued momentum in wearable computing and mixed-reality hardware.

items.title: Spotify adds verification badges for podcasts
items.summary: Spotify is rolling out verification badges for podcasts in an effort to help listeners identify legitimate shows. The move follows similar verification steps the company has taken for music.

items.title: Researchers report sign language translation system using sensor rings
items.summary: Researchers in South Korea have developed a sign language translation system that uses users wearing seven sensor-equipped rings. The work could improve accessibility tools and human-computer interaction.

items.title: Scientists demonstrate scalable carbon nanotube fiber manufacturing
items.summary: Spanish researchers say they have developed a scalable way to manufacture carbon nanotube fibers with conductivity comparable to copper and aluminum. If validated, the process could have implications for advanced electronics and materials manufacturing.
